changes_made: Renamed 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' to 'k8s_audit_logging_configured' for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are applicable as they relate to ensuring actions in Kubernetes can be traced back to authenticated and authorized identities, which aligns with the non-repudiation control. Audit logging and RBAC are key components in achieving this. However, minor naming adjustments were made for clarity and consistency.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the check being performed and adhere to the pattern k8s_<resource>_<check_description>
title: Non-Repudiation - Non - repudiation | validate binding of information producer identity (a) valida
step1_reasoning: The control AU-10(2) focuses on non-repudiation by validating the binding of information producer identity. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through comprehensive audit logging to ensure all actions are recorded and can be traced back to the initiating identity. Enabling audit logging and configuring retention policies ensures that logs are available for forensic analysis. Additionally, ensuring that the API server has authentication and RBAC authorization enabled helps in binding actions to authenticated and authorized identities.
